 {"id":"beads_rust-6z0w","title":"Define named graph health threshold constants in mcp/resources.rs","description":"SUBTASK 4.4: src/mcp/resources.rs contains several inline business-logic thresholds used for interpreting graph health metrics. These should be named constants with documenting comments explaining the interpretation.

SPECIFIC LOCATIONS:

1. Line 760: .filter(|(_, targets)| targets.len() >= 3)
   Add: const HIGH_FAN_OUT_THRESHOLD: usize = 3; // Issues blocking 3+ others are high-fan-out

2. Lines 765, 803: chrono::Duration::days(30) and "stale_threshold_days": 30
   Add: const STALE_THRESHOLD_DAYS: i64 = 30; // Issues not updated in 30+ days are stale

3. Lines 783-787: density > 0.5 and density > 0.2
   Add: const DENSITY_VERY_HIGH: f64 = 0.5; // Above this, issues are heavily coupled
   Add: const DENSITY_MODERATE: f64 = 0.2;  // Above this, some coupling worth reviewing

4. Lines 793-795: max_chain_depth > 5 and max_chain_depth > 2
   Add: const CHAIN_DEPTH_DEEP: usize = 5;    // Critical path is long
   Add: const CHAIN_DEPTH_MODERATE: usize = 2; // Moderate chain depth

REASONING: These thresholds are graph-analysis heuristics. Having them as named constants with comments makes the interpretation criteria transparent and tunable. An agent or developer can adjust these based on project size and complexity.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:58.442981341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:28.328872164Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:28.328416761Z","close_reason":"All 6 graph health thresholds named in mcp/resources.rs: HIGH_FAN_OUT_THRESHOLD, STALE_THRESHOLD_DAYS, DENSITY_VERY_HIGH, DENSITY_MODERATE, CHAIN_DEPTH_DEEP, CHAIN_DEPTH_MODERATE.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-72y","title":"Feature: Model Types - Issue, Status, IssueType","description":"# Model Types (Issue / Dependency / Comment / Event)

## Purpose
Define Rust model structs matching classic bd JSON shape and schema. Must be JSONL-compatible and omit gastown fields.

## Issue Fields (classic)
Include all fields in `EXISTING_BEADS_STRUCTURE_AND_ARCHITECTURE.md`:
- Identification: `id`, `content_hash` (json:-)
- Content: `title`, `description`, `design`, `acceptance_criteria`, `notes`
- Workflow: `status`, `priority`, `issue_type`
- Assignment: `assignee`, `owner`, `estimated_minutes`
- Timestamps: `created_at`, `created_by`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, `close_reason`, `closed_by_session`
- Scheduling: `due_at`, `defer_until`
- External: `external_ref`, `source_system`
- Compaction: `compaction_level`, `compacted_at`, `compacted_at_commit`, `original_size`
- Tombstone: `deleted_at`, `deleted_by`, `delete_reason`, `original_type`
- Messaging: `sender`, `ephemeral`
- Context: `pinned`, `is_template`
- Relations for export: `labels`, `dependencies`, `comments`

## Status Enum
Classic statuses: `open`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `deferred`, `closed`, `tombstone`, `pinned`.
Custom statuses allowed via config.

## IssueType Enum
Classic types: `task`, `bug`, `feature`, `epic`, `chore`, `docs`, `question`.
Custom types allowed via config.

## Dependency
Fields: `issue_id`, `depends_on_id`, `type`, `created_at`, `created_by`, `metadata`, `thread_id`.
Dependency types (classic):
- Blocking: `blocks`, `parent-child`, `conditional-blocks`, `waits-for`
- Informational: `related`, `discovered-from`, `replies-to`, `relates-to`, `duplicates`, `supersedes`, `caused-by`

## Comment
`id`, `issue_id`, `author`, `text`, `created_at`.

## Event
`id`, `issue_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `comment`, `created_at`.

## Serde Rules
- Use `omitempty`/`skip_serializing_if` to match bd JSONL.
- Arrays must serialize as `[]` (not null).
- Time fields are RFC3339 strings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shape matches bd for classic fields.
- Gastown/HOP fields excluded.

## Tests
- Serialize/deserialize fixtures from bd JSONL.
- Validate default values and omitempty behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:07.484948678Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:37:00.821566933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:37:00.821566933Z","close_reason":"Implemented core types and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
-{"id":"beads_rust-75gh","title":"[LOW] README verify-installation version output is stale","description":"Root cause: README.md:324-329 shows `br --version` output as `br 0.1.0 (rustc 1.85.0-nightly)`, but the implementation and current package metadata emit the package version only for `--version` and richer build metadata through `br version`. Evidence: Cargo.toml:3 is version 0.1.45, src/cli/commands/version.rs:117 builds `br version {version} ({build})`, and local `RUST_LOG=error br --version` prints `br 0.1.45`. Repro: run `RUST_LOG=error br --version` and compare README.md:327-328. Proposed fix: update the README verify-installation snippet to match current `br --version`, and optionally add a second `br version` example for build metadata. Test to add: docs snapshot that README version examples do not pin obsolete versions or rustc strings.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:54.298598588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:54.298598588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"beads_rust-7eiw","title":"[P2] Fuzz: content_hash harness generates issues without validation gate","description":"Gap: fuzz/fuzz_targets/content_hash.rs constructs Issue structs from raw fuzz bytes via issue_from_bytes() (using ByteCursor helpers from common.rs) and computes content hashes on them without any validation. The harness verifies hash determinism, JSON formatting stability, unknown field tolerance, empty optional equivalence, NUL-space equivalence, and ignored fields — all important hash invariants — but does not verify that the generated issues are valid according to IssueValidator::validate(). This means the harness may be computing hashes on issues with invalid IDs, oversized titles, out-of-range priorities, or broken timestamp invariants. If content_hash() behaves differently on invalid vs valid issues (e.g., panics on certain field combinations), the harness won't detect it. Target fuzz harness: content_hash. Invariant to assert: partition fuzz-generated issues into valid (pass IssueValidator::validate()) and invalid; verify hash properties hold for both partitions but especially that valid issues always produce a well-formed 64-char hex hash. Compare with markdown_import.rs:34 which already demonstrates the validate-then-hash pattern.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_3","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:51.142898784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:22.468229884Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":324,"issue_id":"beads_rust-7eiw","author":"cod_3","text":"degraded-coordination: files: fuzz/fuzz_targets/content_hash.rs, .beads/issues.jsonl","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:22Z"}]}

This means the harness may be computing hashes on issues with invalid IDs, oversized titles, out-of-range priorities, or broken timestamp invariants. Compare with markdown_import.rs:34 which already demonstrates the validate-then-hash pattern.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_3","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:51.142898784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:13.919309459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":324,"issue_id":"beads_rust-7eiw","author":"cod_3","text":"degraded-coordination: files: fuzz/fuzz_targets/content_hash.rs, .beads/issues.jsonl","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:22Z"},{"id":325,"issue_id":"beads_rust-7eiw","author":"cod_3","text":"verification-blocked: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cod_3 cargo +nightly fuzz run content_hash -- -runs=3000 fails before running content_hash because path dependency /data/projects/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs:8628 has a dirty compile error: Expr::In pattern is matching &Box<Expr> instead of Expr.  {"id":"beads_rust-7j65","title":"Avoid placeholder storage after VACUUM INTO reopen failure","description":"mock-code-finder audit: src/config/mod.rs compact_database_via_vacuum_into_in_place() can leave the caller with the throwaway in-memory placeholder if reopening db_path fails after a successful VACUUM INTO rename. That is documented in the comment and logged, but it means subsequent same-process work may operate on an empty placeholder handle instead of failing or reopening the real DB. Fix should return/surface an error or otherwise prevent live commands from continuing on placeholder storage, with an error-path test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:58:45.290797033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:39:01.083431280Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:39:01.083124366Z","close_reason":"Propagated VACUUM INTO reopen failures and made compaction return a valid reopened storage handle instead of leaving placeholder storage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-7kme","title":"EPIC: Test Infrastructure Enhancements","description":"# EPIC: Test Infrastructure Enhancements

 {"id":"beads_rust-ir0t","title":"Scenario DSL + normalization rules for conformance","description":"Define a scenario registry/DSL so each test case can drive E2E, conformance, and benchmark modes from the same script.

Scope
- Scenario struct: setup (dataset or fresh), commands (argv + env), expected invariants, and expected JSON shapes.
- Normalization rules for conformance: stable sorting, ignore volatile fields (timestamps, randomized ids) where needed, and compare semantics (counts, statuses, deps).
- Shared assertions for “no git ops”, path confinement, and deterministic JSON output.

Acceptance
- Scenarios can be executed in modes: E2E (br only), Conformance (br vs bd), Benchmark (timing + RSS).
- Normalization is explicit and logged when applied.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueStream","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:47.932670110Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:32:24.020248596Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:32:24.020248596Z","close_reason":"Scenario DSL complete: Scenario, ScenarioCommand, ScenarioResult, ScenarioRunner, NormalizationRules, ScenarioFilter all implemented with E2E/Conformance/Benchmark modes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7wqg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Normalization guidance: for read-only commands expect byte-for-byte JSON equality after stable sort; for mutating commands compare structural invariants (status/priority/labels/deps) and allow timestamp skew. Log any field-level ignore so conformance diffs remain explainable.","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ithz","title":"Align sidecar health classification with frankensqlite WAL semantics","description":"Code review found src/health.rs still classifies a WAL sidecar without SHM as SidecarMismatch/degraded even though doctor.rs treats WAL-without-SHM as expected for frankensqlite. This makes startup/OpenStorageResult classification and doctor health disagree. Update the canonical file classifier to tolerate WAL-only, flag SHM-only, and keep doctor anomaly details accurate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:56:40.468954384Z","created_by":"VioletStream","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:39.896770911Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:39.896413452Z","close_reason":"Canonical health classification now treats WAL-without-SHM as expected for frankensqlite, flags SHM-without-WAL, and doctor sidecar anomalies preserve accurate WAL/SHM presence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ivce","title":"Integrate rich output into create command","description":"## Command: br create <TITLE>

### Traffic Level: HIGH
Issue creation - needs clear confirmation feedback.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/create.rs
Output: 'Created ID: title' single line

### Integration Steps
1. After successful creation, render mini IssuePanel
2. Show created issue with all metadata
3. Highlight what was auto-assigned (defaults)
4. If --dry-run, show preview panel with 'DRY RUN' badge

### Success Feedback
```
✓ Created beads_rust-abc1

╭─ beads_rust-abc1 ──────────────────────────────────╮
│  Fix authentication timeout bug                     │
│                                                     │
│  Status     open          Priority   P2 (default)   │
│  Type       task          Owner      @you           │
│  Labels     (none)                                  │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Dry Run Display
```
╭─ DRY RUN ───────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  Would create issue with:                           │
│                                                     │
│  Title      Fix authentication timeout bug          │
│  Status     open          Priority   P2             │
│  Type       task                                    │
│                                                     │
│  Run without --dry-run to create                    │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Agent Mode (--silent)
With --silent flag, output only the ID:
```
beads_rust-abc1
```
No panel, no confirmation text. Just the ID for piping.

### JSON Mode
Output full issue object, same as before.

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/create_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_create_shows_success_panel() {
    let issue = create_issue_from_args(CreateArgs::default());
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_create_success(&issue, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Created"));
    assert!(output.contains(&issue.id));
}

#[test]
fn test_create_json_output_unchanged() {
    let result = run_create_json("Test title");
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/create.json");
    // JSON structure should match (values will differ)
    assert_json_schema_eq!(result, baseline);
}

#[test]
fn test_create_dry_run_shows_preview() {
    let args = CreateArgs { dry_run: true, ..Default::default() };
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_create_preview(&args, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("DRY RUN"));
    assert!(output.contains("Would create"));
}

#[test]
fn test_create_silent_mode_outputs_id_only() {
    let issue = create_test_issue();
    let ctx = OutputContext::from_flags(false, true); // silent
    let output = render_create_success(&issue, &ctx);
    assert_eq!(output.trim(), issue.id);
}

#[test]
fn test_create_shows_default_annotations() {
    // When priority is defaulted, should show "(default)"
    let issue = create_issue_with_defaults();
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_create_success(&issue, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("(default)"));
}
```

### Integration Tests
Location: tests/integration/create_integration.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_create_command_basic() {
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["create", "Test issue", "--dry-run"])
        .output();
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_create_with_all_options() {
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["create", "Test", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--dry-run"])
        .output();
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_create_silent_mode() {
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["create", "Silent test", "--silent", "--dry-run"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let output = String::from_utf8_lossy(&result.stdout);
    // Should be just the ID (or DRY RUN message)
    assert!(output.lines().count() <= 1);
}

#[test]
fn test_create_json_mode() {
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["create", "JSON test", "--json", "--dry-run"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let _: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&result.stdout).unwrap();
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/create_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Create Command ==="

# Setup
setup_test_db

# Test 1: Basic create (dry run)
log_step "Testing basic create (dry run)"
DRY_OUTPUT=$(br create "Test issue" --dry-run 2>&1)
assert_contains "$DRY_OUTPUT" "DRY RUN" "Should show dry run mode"
log_pass "Dry run works"

# Test 2: Actual create
log_step "Testing actual create"
CREATE_OUTPUT=$(br create "E2E Test Issue" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$CREATE_OUTPUT" "Created" "Should confirm creation"
CREATED_ID=$(echo "$CREATE_OUTPUT" | grep -oE 'beads_rust-[a-z0-9]+' | head -1)
assert_not_empty "$CREATED_ID" "Should return issue ID"
log_pass "Basic create works, ID: $CREATED_ID"

# Test 3: Silent mode outputs only ID
log_step "Testing silent mode"
SILENT_OUTPUT=$(br create "Silent test" --silent 2>&1)
# Should be just the ID
assert_lines_count "$SILENT_OUTPUT" 1 "Silent mode should output one line"
assert_match "$SILENT_OUTPUT" "^beads_rust-[a-z0-9]+$" "Should be just the ID"
log_pass "Silent mode works"

# Test 4: JSON output
log_step "Testing JSON output"
JSON_OUTPUT=$(br create "JSON test" --json 2>&1)
JSON_ID=$(echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.id')
assert_not_empty "$JSON_ID" "JSON should have id field"
log_pass "JSON output works"

# Test 5: Create with options
log_step "Testing create with options"
OPTS_OUTPUT=$(br create "Bug fix" --type bug --priority 1 --label backend --silent 2>&1)
OPTS_ID=$(echo "$OPTS_OUTPUT" | tr -d '[:space:]')
# Verify the issue has correct properties
ISSUE_JSON=$(br show "$OPTS_ID" --json 2>&1)
ISSUE_TYPE=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r '.issue_type')
assert_eq "$ISSUE_TYPE" "bug" "Type should be bug"
log_pass "Create with options works"

# Test 6: Rich mode panel
log_step "Testing rich mode panel"
RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br create "Rich test" --dry-run 2>&1 || true)
# Should have box drawing characters
log_pass "Rich mode renders"

log_success "=== Create command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log create command with all arguments
- Log generated issue ID
- Log which values were defaulted vs specified
- Log dry run mode
- Log rendering mode

 {"id":"beads_rust-j57","title":"audit Command (interactions.jsonl)","description":"# audit Command (interactions.jsonl)

## Purpose
Optional agent interaction logging to `.beads/interactions.jsonl`, matching bd audit schemas. Read/write only; no daemon required.

## CLI
```
br audit record [--kind ... --issue-id ... --model ... --prompt ... --response ... --tool-name ... --exit-code ... --error ... --stdin]
br audit label <entry-id> --label <label> [--reason <text>]
```

## Storage
- Append-only JSONL at `.beads/interactions.jsonl`.
- Entry IDs prefixed `int-` (random 4 bytes, hex).

## JSON Shapes
- `record` output: `{ "id": "int-...", "kind": "..." }`
- `label` output: `{ "id": "...", "parent_id": "...", "label": "..." }`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Appends valid JSONL entries with RFC3339 timestamps.
- Accepts stdin JSON when `--stdin` or no explicit fields.

## Tests
- Record + label output shapes.
- File append order preserved.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"opus_main","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:36.926687577Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:52:27.153121548Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:52:27.153077976Z","close_reason":"audit command fully implemented: record subcommand supports --kind, --issue-id, --model, --prompt, --response, --tool-name, --exit-code, --error, --stdin flags. label subcommand implemented.  {"id":"beads_rust-j804","title":"Define DEFAULT_ID_PREFIX const and consolidate 4 occurrences","description":"SUBTASK 6.1: The default ID prefix "br" appears in 4 separate locations:

1. src/util/id.rs:29 — IdConfig::default(): prefix: "br".into()
2. src/util/id.rs:506 — normalize_prefix(): fallback to "br"
3. src/util/id.rs:575 — ResolverConfig::default(): default_prefix: "br".into()
4. src/config/mod.rs:1816 — default_config_layer(): issue_prefix: Some("br".to_string())

CHANGES:
- Add in src/util/id.rs (near MAX_ID_PREFIX_LEN): pub const DEFAULT_ID_PREFIX: &str = "br";
- Update all 4 locations to use DEFAULT_ID_PREFIX instead of the bare "br" string
- config/mod.rs imports it as: use crate::util::id::DEFAULT_ID_PREFIX;

WHY util/id.rs: The ID module is the authoritative owner of ID-related constants (MAX_ID_PREFIX_LEN, MAX_ID_HASH_LEN already live there). The config module is a consumer of this default.

TESTING: Verify that cargo test still passes — the default prefix affects ID generation in many tests.
